sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! LEFT/RIGHT actions don't change the purple bar (stayed at 12). The purple bar seems to only increase with MOUSE clicks (or maybe it's a fixed limit of 8 clicks).
I see 3 purple nodes at the top, each with 12 pixels. These are likely the purple blocks with white centers that I saw in the image.
Now I understand the structure better:
- 3 purple blocks at top (row ~8, cols 15, 21, 27)
- 14 green blocks in a 2x7 grid
- Player at bottom
The game over happens after 8 MOUSE clicks. I need to collect 14 green blocks. This means I need a way to collect more than 8 blocks.
Maybe the mechanic is:
1. Click a purple block to "activate" something
2. Then collect green blocks
3. Repeat for each purple block
Or maybe clicking the purple blocks does something different.
Let me try clicking one of the purple blocks at the top to see what happens.
Actually, looking at the purple nodes more carefully:
- 3 purple nodes, each with 12 pixels
- They're at the top of the board (row 8)
- They have white centers (from the image)
These might be "power-ups" or "keys" that allow me to collect more green blocks.
Let me try clicking one purple block and see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: LEFT/RIGHT don't affect purple bar. 3 purple blocks at top (row 8, cols 15/21/27). Need to understand their role. Let me try clicking one.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
# Find purple blocks at top (not the bar)
purple_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
print(f"Purple nodes: {len(purple_nodes)}")
for pn in purple_nodes:
    boundary = pn['boundary']
    center_row = sum(p[0] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
    center_col = sum(p[1] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
    print(f"  pixels={pn['pixels']}, center=({center_row}, {center_col})")

# The purple bar is likely one of these (the one with most pixels or at bottom)
# The blocks at top are at row 8
top_purple = [pn for pn in purple_nodes if sum(p[0] for p in pn['boundary']) // len(pn['boundary']) < 20]
print(f"Top purple blocks: {len(top_purple)}")

# Try clicking the first purple block
if top_purple:
    pn = top_purple[0]
    boundary = pn['boundary']
    center_row = sum(p[0] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
    center_col = sum(p[1] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
    print(f"Clicking purple block at ({center_row}, {center_col})")
    
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_row, 'col': center_col}])
    print(f"Result: game_over={result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
    pink = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
    purple_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
    print(f"After: greens={len(greens)}, pink={len(pink)}, purple_nodes={len(purple_nodes)}")
    for pn in purple_nodes:
        boundary = pn['boundary']
        center_row = sum(p[0] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
        center_col = sum(p[1] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
        print(f"  pixels={pn['pixels']}, center=({center_row}, {center_col})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
